titleStmt.title: Letter from John Nevin to the Longford Urban District Council, 8 February 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Nevin

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from John Nevin to the Longford Urban District Council in which Nevin asks for his yearly allocation of funds for clothes as the weather is becoming very cold. John Nevin (b. 1866) was the Town Sergeant for Longford. The Longford Urban District Council was the towns local authority and was renamed the Longford Town Council in 2002.

correspAction.placeName: Longford Urban District Council, Longford, Ireland

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Feby 8th 1916 Gentlemen The weather being very cold and
severe I would feel much obliged to you if you
would allow me the usual yearly sum for
clothes which I need very much — Thanking you
very much for your past kindness to me and
wishing you all a prosperous New Year Your obedient Servant
John Nevin
